name: Mount Nyiragongo
location: East Africa, Democratic Republic of Congo
eruptionHistory: Contains one of the world's rare persistent lava lakes. The 2002 eruption destroyed much of the city of Goma. Erupted again in 2021 killing 32 people.
description: Nyiragongo contains the world's largest lava lake in its summit crater. Its extremely fluid lava can flow at speeds up to 100 km/h, making it one of the most dangerous volcanoes in Africa.
